documented_rules: Card_export / All_Cards_Master match exactly at 754 cards
current_build_snapshot: Build loads Base44 Card entity records
master_resolution: Card_export should remain master source of card facts and membership

documented_rules: 8 families: character, boss, pet, relic, name, edict, site, host_sigil
current_build_snapshot: Same families present in Card entity and UI panels
master_resolution: Use Card_export/Base44 entity values as canonical

documented_rules: 330 character prompt rows, all matched to master cards
current_build_snapshot: Not used directly by gameplay build
master_resolution: Keep as character reference only; no removals needed

documented_rules: mechanics_expanded covers all 664 character+boss cards; mechanics.csv misses 1 boss (Anu the Exarch of Heaven)
current_build_snapshot: Build uses card data plus simplified combat/helpers
master_resolution: Use mechanics_expanded as the complete supplemental mechanics file

documented_rules: Rule docs support 2–4 players
current_build_snapshot: Build UI is arranged for 4 seats and bot seats
master_resolution: Document build as 4-player digital baseline; keep 2–4 as ruleset capability

documented_rules: Unified rulebook: exactly 36 cards
current_build_snapshot: Build does not appear to enforce deck construction inside gameplay page
master_resolution: Retain 36-card legality in master rules, mark deck validation as external/lobby concern

documented_rules: Unified rulebook and playthrough: 6 cards
current_build_snapshot: Build hand limit constant is 6; draw phase fills back to 6
master_resolution: Master doc should distinguish opening hand 6 and current digital draw-to-6 behavior

documented_rules: Docs: Sinew 2, Sigil 1, Oath 1
current_build_snapshot: Build initializes resources as 2/1/1
master_resolution: Keep 2/1/1

documented_rules: Playthrough/build/entity use 7 phases: Upkeep, Draw, Edicts, Main, Battle/Battles, Capture, End; unified rulebook compresses to 5 phases
current_build_snapshot: Build uses 7-phase flow
master_resolution: Use 7-phase digital master; treat unified 5-phase version as compressed tabletop wording

documented_rules: Unified rulebook grants +1/+1/+1 in Upkeep
current_build_snapshot: Current build grants all three resources in End phase
master_resolution: Master doc should show current build behavior separately and flag target rules behavior

documented_rules: Unified rulebook: draw 1 in Draw phase
current_build_snapshot: Build fills hand to HAND_LIMIT (6) during Draw
master_resolution: Document as current build simplification, not canonical target

documented_rules: Unified/playthrough: Names and Edicts are distinct law/event actions; Names raise Audit Heat
current_build_snapshot: Build Edicts phase allows name/edict and adds +1 Audit Heat for edict context
master_resolution: Keep separate Edicts phase; clarify Name heat and one-shot Edict resolution in master doc

documented_rules: Docs: Characters, Bosses, Pets, Relics, Sites, Host Sigils in Main
current_build_snapshot: Build PhasePanel matches these families in Main
master_resolution: Keep current family deployment map

documented_rules: Docs define Jurisdiction Fields, Line crossing, Geasa, field limits
current_build_snapshot: Build data model references jurisdiction_fields and tutorial text, but active rules look minimally enforced in page logic
master_resolution: Retain full rules in master mechanics and note implementation gap

documented_rules: Unified rulebook: Stone, Veil, Oath are core axes
current_build_snapshot: Build combat UI uses Stone, Veil, Oath
master_resolution: Keep Stone/Veil/Oath as core

documented_rules: Unified/playthrough: supporters, resource boosts, modifiers, consequences, deterministic totals
current_build_snapshot: Build attack currently compares chosen axis value only and downs defender on win
master_resolution: Master doc should preserve full deterministic combat model and separately note simplified build implementation

documented_rules: Unified: 2 Wounds = Downed
current_build_snapshot: Build appears to down target immediately on winning attack
master_resolution: Keep 2-wound target rules as canonical target; note build simplification

documented_rules: Unified: Bind requires Downed + Oath/Sanctuary conditions; Bosses need 3 Trial counters
current_build_snapshot: Build capture awards marks from downed units; no full bind/trial enforcement visible in page logic
master_resolution: Preserve Bind/Trial/Boss capture rules in master doc and mark as partially implemented

documented_rules: Docs: caused by law manipulation and risky effects; audit check at 5+
current_build_snapshot: Build has thresholds at 3/5/7/9 and edict/name heat handling plus tracker UI
master_resolution: Master doc should include current threshold ladder and keep audit check concepts

documented_rules: Unified/playthrough define Name arbitration with hidden Oath bids
current_build_snapshot: Build tutorial references arbitration, but no full implementation is obvious in gameplay page
master_resolution: Keep rules in master doc as not yet fully implemented

documented_rules: Unified: 10 standard marks, alt 6/12 modes, +1 char, +2 boss, +1 seize Name, +1 Site raid
current_build_snapshot: Build ends at 10 marks; capture grants 1 or 2 marks by rarity
master_resolution: Keep 10-mark standard; note build currently scores by capture rarity instead of full event model

documented_rules: Unified End: discard to 9 and optionally discard to lower Audit Heat
current_build_snapshot: Build enforces discard to 6 before End and currently uses End for resource collection
master_resolution: Document current build behavior separately from target rules

documented_rules: Canonical rules now define axis identities, consequence chains, one narrow reaction window, field hooks, and support-generated Heat.
current_build_snapshot: Current build resolves simplified deterministic axis comparison without full reaction, chain, or terrain hook layers.
master_resolution: Use Canonical Master Rules v1.0 as authority; treat full expressive combat as target implementation.

documented_rules: Fields now carry control state and End-step economy hooks such as Sanctuary Sigil gain, Warfield Stone pressure, Threshold retreat relief, and Wilds dampening.
current_build_snapshot: Current build shows field presence but not full control-economy resolution.
master_resolution: Preserve digital field shell, but canonicalize control-state and field-economy rules in master docs.

documented_rules: Heat now supports banking, conversion into power, threshold riders, and archetype-level pressure beyond simple ladder display.
current_build_snapshot: Current build visibly tracks the audit ladder but does not fully expose banking/conversion or expanded threshold play.
master_resolution: Keep build ladder values; expand gameplay authority to full Heat risk engine from master rules.

documented_rules: Arbitration now includes sealed bids, witnesses, precedent tokens, injunctions, counter-claims, and seizure/suppression/corruption outcomes.
current_build_snapshot: Current build and prior docs preserve only the Oath-bid contest skeleton.
master_resolution: Canonicalize expanded Arbitration as the full legal-control subsystem.

documented_rules: Bosses now include stance states, aura laws, raid-like pressure, and trial retaliation in addition to upkeep and Trials.
current_build_snapshot: Current build supports Boss cards but not the full encounter-style subsystem.
master_resolution: Treat encounter-style Boss behavior as canonical target rules.

documented_rules: Scenario modifiers are now official replayability tools that adjust the match without replacing core rules.
current_build_snapshot: No evidence of implemented scenario-modifier support in the current build snapshot.
master_resolution: Canonicalize scenario modifiers as format-level optional rules.

documented_rules: Field dominance, Trial counters, audit escalation, Name ownership, and capture/bind/raid eligibility are now mandatory visible state markers.
current_build_snapshot: Current build surfaces some state but not the full required visibility layer.
master_resolution: Treat visibility requirements as gameplay-authority requirements for future implementation.

documented_rules: Shipping canon now defines 2-, 3-, and 4-player support as first-class modes, with >4 only if validated.
current_build_snapshot: Current build visibly supports a 4-seat presentation and phase-ready sync, but doctrine previously still contained optional-extension wording.
master_resolution: Treat 2–4 player support as canonical shipping scope; remove optional-extension interpretation from live doctrine and complete rules/UI implementation.

documented_rules: Canonical target rules use a normal draw-1 model after setup unless altered by card or mode.
current_build_snapshot: Current build Draw phase refills toward hand limit 6.
master_resolution: Record refill-to-6 as the current implementation snapshot; keep draw-1 as target canon until implementation is intentionally changed or canon is revised.

documented_rules: Target canon defines expressive deterministic combat with support, spend windows, narrow reaction window, consequence choice, and chain logic.
current_build_snapshot: Current build uses a simplified deterministic axis-comparison combat flow with downing and capture follow-through.
master_resolution: Treat simplified combat as current build reality; maintain expressive combat as target canon and reconciliation target.

documented_rules: Final Gameplay Doctrine, Final Engineering Doctrine, and Shipping Gaps now form the active authority stack.
current_build_snapshot: Some bridge/reference framing still reflected older consolidation language prior to shipping update.
master_resolution: Use the finalized doctrine hierarchy as the active source-precedence model for all future work.

documented_rules: Shipping requires 2–4 player support with UI legibility and visible state clarity.
current_build_snapshot: Current build already shows a 4-seat multiplayer presentation with phase-ready indicators, but full state-visibility compliance remains incomplete.
master_resolution: Use the existing 4-seat presentation as the baseline implementation shell; complete the required visibility and interaction layer for shipping.
